## Description

[![@ikki4](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/38467650?u=dd5242069e96890fd946a3fe926b9c2c22807945&v=4&size=48)](https://github.com/ikki4)

opened [on May 17, 2023on May 17, 2023](https://github.com/qbittorrent/qBittorrent/issues/18988#issue-1714272586)

Issue body actions

### Suggestion

Option to send a notification instead of auto downloading a torrent that matches the rule definition. Could be inside the "Rule Definition" area of the RSS Auto Downloader.

![qbittorrent_2023-05-17_13-05-23](https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/38467650/239017865-bcdb358e-2f8d-492e-861f-3992923d2b5d.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..s0l1tG0NdkrRr0ntc2FkpwywjBWqIK1djtht6YeoxrI)

### Use case

Let's say that a certain private tracker's RSS feed does not specify in the titles information such as language, resolution and freeleech status. This means that this RSS feed will have multiple titles with the same name (because of multiple different releases), and the Auto Downloader would probably try to download them all, but that's not what the average user would want, in this case.

If instead of auto downloading we get a notification, then the user can better decide which release to download, whether by accessing the tracker's website, or by selecting each title in the qBittorrent's RSS feed (to show extra info on the right side).

(I know that this could be "solved" by using another RSS reader with notification options, but it would be nice to not have to use/install another program/service)

### Extra info/examples/attachments

_No response_

👍React with 👍2tainewoo and dm-vodopyanov

How does the notification help you decide?

In either case (with or without notifications) this is a manual operation or its automatic via RSS rules.

One thing you can do with existing capabilities is add them all in a Paused state. Then come back and choose the titles you want and delete the rest.

[![ikki4](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/38467650?u=dd5242069e96890fd946a3fe926b9c2c22807945&v=4&size=80)](https://github.com/ikki4)

> How does the notification help you decide?

> In either case (with or without notifications) this is a manual operation or its automatic via RSS rules.

Yeah, I guess I didn't word that very well, my bad. The notification wouldn't help me decide, it would alert me that the desired torrent is available and that I can manually go choose a release to download. And as I said, this can be done by using another RSS reader that has RegEx + notification capabilities (like fluent-reader), but since qBit has its own basic RSS reader with RegEx capabilities, I just thought that this notification feature could be implemented in it aswell. But I know it has more to do with RSS than with torrenting, downloading or auto-downloading.

> One thing you can do with existing capabilities is add them all in a Paused state. Then come back and choose the titles you want and delete the rest.

Yeah, I thought about this after posting this suggestion, and after founding out about the option to activate notifications for all added torrents (in the advanced options).

As I said, I now realize that the feature I suggested has more to do with RSS functionality than with torrenting, so if you feel like qBit doesn't need that, feel free to close this issue right away.

[select all](https://community.home-assistant.io/t/qbittorrent-pass-name-of-last-torrent-started-to-input-text-helper/799668) [cancel selecting](https://community.home-assistant.io/t/qbittorrent-pass-name-of-last-torrent-started-to-input-text-helper/799668) 760 views [![](https://community.home-assistant.io/user_avatar/community.home-assistant.io/roadkilluk/48/513203_2.png)4](https://community.home-assistant.io/u/RoadkillUK "RoadkillUK") [![](https://community.home-assistant.io/letter_avatar/mkjustuk/48/5_047764fa20c941da5a8a2a620d7322fb.png)4](https://community.home-assistant.io/u/mkjustuk "mkjustuk") [Nov 2024](https://community.home-assistant.io/t/qbittorrent-pass-name-of-last-torrent-started-to-input-text-helper/799668/1 "Jump to the first post") 2 / 8 Nov 2024 [Nov 2024](https://community.home-assistant.io/t/qbittorrent-pass-name-of-last-torrent-started-to-input-text-helper/799668/8) [![](https://community.home-assistant.io/letter_avatar/mkjustuk/48/5_047764fa20c941da5a8a2a620d7322fb.png)](https://community.home-assistant.io/u/mkjustuk) [mkjustuk](https://community.home-assistant.io/u/mkjustuk) 1 [Nov 2024](https://community.home-assistant.io/t/qbittorrent-pass-name-of-last-torrent-started-to-input-text-helper/799668 "Post date") I am having a really hard time trying to get a working automation that will show the name of the last torrent started in qbit. I have read the documentation but the action seems only half explained. Does anyone do this and can share their yaml? End goal is to trigger another automation if the name of the last torrent to start downloading contained ABC 760 views [![](https://community.home-assistant.io/user_avatar/community.home-assistant.io/roadkilluk/48/513203_2.png)4](https://community.home-assistant.io/u/RoadkillUK "RoadkillUK") [![](https://community.home-assistant.io/letter_avatar/mkjustuk/48/5_047764fa20c941da5a8a2a620d7322fb.png)4](https://community.home-assistant.io/u/mkjustuk "mkjustuk") [![](https://community.home-assistant.io/user_avatar/community.home-assistant.io/roadkilluk/48/513203_2.png)](https://community.home-assistant.io/u/RoadkillUK) [RoadkillUK](https://community.home-assistant.io/u/RoadkillUK) [Nov 2024](https://community.home-assistant.io/t/qbittorrent-pass-name-of-last-torrent-started-to-input-text-helper/799668/2 "Post date") There is a way to send a webhook from Qbittorrent to HA. I’ll have to wait 'til I get home to take a look at it as it’s in a batch file that automatically runs after torrent added. ![:crossed_fingers:](https://community.home-assistant.io/images/emoji/twitter/crossed_fingers.png?v=12) If I remember ![:slight_smile:](https://community.home-assistant.io/images/emoji/twitter/slight_smile.png?v=12) [![](https://community.home-assistant.io/user_avatar/community.home-assistant.io/roadkilluk/48/513203_2.png)](https://community.home-assistant.io/u/RoadkillUK) [RoadkillUK](https://community.home-assistant.io/u/RoadkillUK) [Nov 2024](https://community.home-assistant.io/t/qbittorrent-pass-name-of-last-torrent-started-to-input-text-helper/799668/3 "Post date") OK, in Qbittorrent, Options, Downloads. Check _‘x’ “Run external program on torrent finished”_, then in that field, put “C:\\downloads\\notify.bat %N”. \* **I just thought, you probably won’t need a batch file, just put the curl command instead of the above notify.bat**, but I’ve typed all this so it’s staying. Then make that batch file _“notify.bat”_ In the batch file ```swift curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d "{\"torrent_name\":\"%1\"}" http://[YOUR-IP]:8123/api/webhook/qbittorrent_torrent_added ``` Don’t forget to add your HA IP address. This is your automation, put notifications or whatever in the actions: ```yaml alias: New Torrent Added description: "" triggers: - trigger: webhook allowed_methods: - POST - PUT local_only: true webhook_id: qbittorrent_torrent_added conditions: [] actions: [YOUR ACTIONS HERE] mode: single ``` [![](https://community.home-assistant.io/letter_avatar/mkjustuk/48/5_047764fa20c941da5a8a2a620d7322fb.png)](https://community.home-assistant.io/u/mkjustuk) [mkjustuk](https://community.home-assistant.io/u/mkjustuk) [Nov 2024](https://community.home-assistant.io/t/qbittorrent-pass-name-of-last-torrent-started-to-input-text-helper/799668/4 "Post date") Awesome, thanks. I’ll have a play with it. Appreciate your time in posting all the above. [![](https://community.home-assistant.io/user_avatar/community.home-assistant.io/roadkilluk/48/513203_2.png)](https://community.home-assistant.io/u/RoadkillUK) [RoadkillUK](https://community.home-assistant.io/u/RoadkillUK) [Nov 2024](https://community.home-assistant.io/t/qbittorrent-pass-name-of-last-torrent-started-to-input-text-helper/799668/5 "Post date") Just had a thought, if you put the line in Qbittorrent and not the batch file, use this instead ( _changed %1 to %N_) ```swift curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d "{\"torrent_name\":\"%N\"}" http://[YOUR-IP]:8123/api/webhook/qbittorrent_torrent_added ``` [![](https://community.home-assistant.io/letter_avatar/mkjustuk/48/5_047764fa20c941da5a8a2a620d7322fb.png)](https://community.home-assistant.io/u/mkjustuk) [mkjustuk](https://community.home-assistant.io/u/mkjustuk) [Nov 2024](https://community.home-assistant.io/t/qbittorrent-pass-name-of-last-torrent-started-to-input-text-helper/799668/6 "Post date") Thanks again, Qbit seems happy with the curl directly, but when it gets to HA it’s throwing an error: ```auto Logger: homeassistant.components.webhook Source: components/webhook/__init__.py:192 integration: Webhook (documentation, issues) First occurred: 25 November 2024 at 21:47:56 (8 occurrences) Last logged: 13:35:46 Error processing webhook qbittorrent_torrent_added Traceback (most recent call last): File "/usr/src/homeassistant/homeassistant/components/webhook/__init__.py", line 192, in async_handle_webhook response: Response | None = await webhook["handler"](hass, webhook_id, request) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ File "/usr/src/homeassistant/homeassistant/components/webhook/trigger.py", line 64, in _handle_webhook base_result["json"] = await request.json() ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ File "/usr/src/homeassistant/homeassistant/components/http/__init__.py", line 307, in json return json_loads(await self.read()) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ File "/usr/src/homeassistant/homeassistant/util/json.py", line 44, in json_loads return orjson.loads(__obj) # type:ignore[no-any-return] ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ orjson.JSONDecodeError: unexpected character: line 1 column 2 (char 1) ``` [![](https://community.home-assistant.io/user_avatar/community.home-assistant.io/roadkilluk/48/513203_2.png)](https://community.home-assistant.io/u/RoadkillUK) [RoadkillUK](https://community.home-assistant.io/u/RoadkillUK) [Nov 2024](https://community.home-assistant.io/t/qbittorrent-pass-name-of-last-torrent-started-to-input-text-helper/799668/7 "Post date") Are you running Qbittorrent on Windows or Linux? My command is for Windows, try this instead. ```nginx curl -v -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"torrent_name":"%N"}' http://[YOUR-IP]:8123/api/webhook/qbittorrent_torrent_added ``` [![](https://community.home-assistant.io/letter_avatar/mkjustuk/48/5_047764fa20c941da5a8a2a620d7322fb.png)](https://community.home-assistant.io/u/mkjustuk) [mkjustuk](https://community.home-assistant.io/u/mkjustuk) [Nov 2024](https://community.home-assistant.io/t/qbittorrent-pass-name-of-last-torrent-started-to-input-text-helper/799668/8 "Post date") Thanks for all your help again. I am running qbit in a docker container on Debian. It took some iterations, but I finally got it working. I couldn’t get a curl command to work directly in qbit, so ended up with a bash script. Even then, any \_ would cause an issue for some reason. For anyone else that might find this, the final solution for me was as follows: In qbit, tick the run external option in downloads and add this: ```bash /config/ha.sh "%N" ``` Then in the qbit config folder add the file ha.sh with the following contents: ```nginx curl -v POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"torrent":"'"$1"'"}' http://[YOUR IP ADDRESS]:8123/api/webhook/qbittorrent_torrent_added ``` In HA you can then have an automation with triggers and actions like these (this one just sends the torrent’s name through to Telegram for example) ```yaml - id: '123' alias: New Torrent Added description: "Torrent added" triggers: - trigger: webhook allowed_methods: - POST - PUT local_only: true webhook_id: "qbittorrent_torrent_added" action: - data: message: "{{ trigger.json.torrent }}" service: notify.telegram_me mode: single ``` Reply ### New & Unread Topics | Topic | Replies | Views | Activity | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | [Automation fails silently, but the exact same service call works from Developer Tools. Messages8Reaction score1

I have version 4.5.2 on my computer and I'm wondering how to get notifications when a download is complete. When I download a torrent, it will just finish eventually but not display any notification in the corner letting me know that it's finished. When I first used qBittorrent a couple years ago, it did have those notifications but now it doesn't seem to want to show them for some reason. I went into Settings > Advanced, and I found the option that said "Display notifications" to be already checked, so I don't know what to do.

If someone could let me know how to actually do this I would greatly appreciate it!

[![Hellbovine](https://www.ntlite.com/community/data/avatars/m/9/9951.jpg?1661369247)](https://www.ntlite.com/community/index.php?members/hellbovine.9951/)

#### [Hellbovine](https://www.ntlite.com/community/index.php?members/hellbovine.9951/)

##### Well-Known Member

Messages1,209Reaction score761

Could be a number of things causing it. This is what I would check first:

1) Start > Settings > System > Notifications & actions > Get notifications from apps and other senders > On

2) Start > Settings > System > Focus assist > Off

3) Start > Settings > Privacy > Notifications > On

4) Start > Settings > Personalization > Taskbar > Show badges on taskbar buttons > On

5) You removed components with NTLite and/or added some tweaks that break notifications on a deeper level.

So this is a problem on your Windows. Several things can cause this.

Make sure Windows Push Notifications User Service (WpnUserService) is not removed and is set to Automatic.

Check these 2 mentioned by Hellbovine (only these 2 are essential, the others are optional or depend on the app but not necessary for qBittorrent):

> 1) Start > Settings > System > Notifications & actions > Get notifications from apps and other senders > On
>
> 2) Start > Settings > System > Focus assist > Off
>
>
> Click to expand...

Go to Settings > System > Notifications & actions scroll to the bottom and make sure that qBittorrent appears there and is enabled.

But it's best to know what you've removed or disabled and what tweaks you've used. Post your preset that you used with NTLite as already mentioned.

An alternative is to use the old Windows notification method (balloon notifications).

Go to NTLite in Settings > Explorer > Ballon notifications (Legacy) and leave it as Enabled. I use it ![:)](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/joypixels/assets/8.0/png/unicode/64/1f642.png)

Or use this reg tweak (I think it's necessary to restart explorer.exe or restart Windows):

Code:


```
Windows Registry Editor Version 5.00

[HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer]
"EnableLegacyBalloonNotifications"=dword:00000001
```

If the Explorer "directory" doesn't exist you have to create it. The name of this is key.

Right click on the Windows "directory" go to New > Key and type Explorer. Then right click in Explorer or in the white space on the right, go to New > DWORD (32-bit) Value. Type EnableLegacyBalloonNotifications and press Enter. Then click 2 times type 1 and click OK.

[View attachment 9847](https://www.ntlite.com/community/index.php?attachments/9847/)

Or copy this below into a text editor (notepad could be) and save it with the .reg extension (Example: EnableBalloonNotifications.reg) and double click on the file.

Code:


```
Windows Registry Editor Version 5.00

[HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer]
"EnableLegacyBalloonNotifications"=dword:00000001
```

Sometimes it's too difficult to troubleshoot something because we just don't know all the miscellaneous tweaks someone has done. There are so many cases of people installing a random optimization script they downloaded and then forgot they did that, or they manually messed with settings and broke something, or some form of file corruption or viruses are on the computer, etcetera.

Save yourself a ton of time and headache and simply download a clean ISO straight from Microsoft and reinstall Windows. This is the solution that fixes just about everything, unless your problems are stemming from a bug in Windows, a driver, or bios.

Check out these beginner guides on how to do this if you don't know the steps:

1) Guide: NTLite for Beginners ( [link](https://www.ntlite.com/community/index.php?threads/guide-ntlite-for-beginners.2979/))

2) Guide: Installing Windows ( [link](https://www.ntlite.com/community/index.php?threads/guide-installing-windows.2986/))

Issue body actions

### qBittorrent & operating system versions

qBittorrent: 4.4.3.1

Operating system: MacOS 10.15.7

### What is the problem?

Hello team,

After downloading and completing a torrent including multiple files, I restart the app and the torrent still shows as complete

However if I do a forced verification, at the end of the verification the torrent status is at 98 or 99%-ish so I need to wait for the download to complete one more time

I'm usually forced to redo these steps a few more times, each time with a smaller amount of data missing, before the torrent finally shows as complete after a last forced verification

Here is a video illustrating the issue: [https://drive.google.com/file/d/1sIDop2IYuoFvJVncb9OCiHqEi66lt4O\_/view?usp=sharing](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1sIDop2IYuoFvJVncb9OCiHqEi66lt4O_/view?usp=sharing)

I think the issue usually happens with torrents including multiple files,

I also use QBittorrent on a PC, and I have not been able to reproduce the issue on Windows so far

![Thumbnail (1920x1080)](https://i.ytimg.com/vi/Wt8jX4KGSWg/maxresdefault.jpg)
# [qBittorrent Stuck On Downloading Metadata (Quick fix)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Wt8jX4KGSWg)

**Visibility**: Public
**Uploaded by**: [Global Success Narratives](http://www.youtube.com/@GlobalSuccessNarratives)
**Uploaded at**: 2025-02-12T00:03:24-08:00
**Published at**: 2025-02-12T00:03:24-08:00
**Length**: 05:51
**Views**: 38452
**Likes**: 345
**Category**: Howto & Style

## Description

```
if your qBittorrent is stuck at downloading metadata then this tutorial will guide you on how to resolve this problem.
Try the following 
Randomize Port used for incoming connections
Update the Network Interface in qBittorrent
Delete the qBittorrent file in the app data
Try changing Alternative Rate Limits to 0 (infinite).
Enable DHT, PeX, and Local Peer Discovery Settings
Enable "Anonymous Mode" in qBittorrent
Uninstall qBittorrent and then reinstall it.
```

> Attempting to call, via qbittorrent's "run external command" functionality, an API call from another container (cross-seed) to check if a torrent can be cross seeded at the torrent's completion.  The command is:  curl -XPOST http://ipaddress:2468/api/webhook --data-urlencode "name=%N".  I was hoping that the LAN\_ADDRESS worked both inbound and outbound - but appears to only impact incoming requests to qbittorrent (for the UI).  Do I have any options?

Q27 worked for me.

VPN\_OUTPUT\_PORTS:  2468

Using my unraid ip address and not the docker hostname.

curl -XPOST http://192.168.2.222:2468/api/webhook --data-urlencode "name=%N"

The cross-seed docker logs confirms search for cross-seeds after torrent download is completed.

the docker container is designed to work in bridge mode, the only alternative to this is to assign the docker container a static ip on a different network to your LAN, setting the container to use a static ip in your LAN (as you have done) will not work.

This makes sense as to why qbittorrent was working with swag.  I checked docker to see what drivers each network was using. And the network driver that was created for swag (based on the spaceinvader video) is using the bridge driver, but the network the NPM container created uses the macvlan driver.  I'm not sure why the NPM container creates one with macvlan, but that is for a discussion in that container thread.

I moved the qbittorent (and NPM) container over to a bridge driver network, and things work as expected.

I am bit confused. Looks like this functional is not really needed: already exists 'run external program on finish' (Preferences -> Downloads -> Run external program on completion)

For future readers (maybe to wiki?):

You can use any application to invoke after torrent finish. Like CURL for HTTP requests.

Real example for Telegram API (you should get your token, know your chat id and initialize conversation with bot):

```
curl -X POST -d chat_id=<CHATID> -d text="%N saved into %D (%Z bytes)" 'https://api.telegram.org/bot<TOKEN>/sendMessage'
```

It sends a notification over bot to your telegram when torrent is downloaded.

[@Chocobo1](https://github.com/Chocobo1) may I ask your opinion? Should I implement it as internal functional or better keep it separately (and close request and add to Wiki)?

[![Chocobo1](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/9395168?u=1e7bf15f9c7e47b437b19066ec28d58d074a028b&v=4&size=80)](https://github.com/Chocobo1)

If only "on completion" is needed, I would opt to do less work (use "Run external program on completion").
